qa_pairs.question: How do I copy files to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` on every node in a multinode job?
qa_pairs.answer: To copy one or more files to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` directory on every node allocated, use the command: `srun --ntasks=$SLURM_NNODES --ntasks-per-node=1 cp file [files...] $SLURM_TMPDIR`.

qa_pairs.question: What command should I use to extract a ZIP archive to `SLURM_TMPDIR` across all nodes in a multinode job?
qa_pairs.answer: To extract a ZIP archive to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` on every node, use the command: `srun --ntasks=$SLURM_NNODES --ntasks-per-node=1 unzip archive.zip -d $SLURM_TMPDIR`.

qa_pairs.question: How can I extract a Tarball archive to `SLURM_TMPDIR` when running a job across multiple nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: You can extract a Tarball archive to the `SLURM_TMPDIR` on every node using the command: `srun --ntasks=$SLURM_NNODES --ntasks-per-node=1 tar -xvf archive.tar.gz -C $SLURM_TMPDIR`.

qa_pairs.question: Why are simple `cp` or `tar -x` commands insufficient for moving data to `SLURM_TMPDIR` in multinode jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: A simple `cp` or `tar -x` will not suffice if a job spans multiple nodes and some data is needed on every node.
